snippets.title: Inspecting state — the three commands you'll run constantly
snippets.code:
git status                # what's modified / staged / untracked
git log --oneline -n 10   # last 10 commits, one per line
git diff                  # unstaged changes
git diff --staged         # what's about to be committed
snippets.explanation: If you're confused about Git, almost always the first move is `git status`.

snippets.title: Staging selectively
snippets.code:
git add src/auth.py                # one file
git add src/                         # a whole directory
git add -p                            # interactive: pick hunks within a file

# Oops, staged the wrong file:
git restore --staged src/secret.env  # unstage (keeps the disk change)
snippets.explanation: `git add -p` is the trick for splitting an unrelated change out of a busy working tree.

snippets.title: Making a commit
snippets.code:
git commit -m "Add login endpoint with rate limit"

# Forgot something? Amend the most-recent commit (only if NOT pushed):
git add forgotten.py
git commit --amend --no-edit

# Empty commit (useful to retrigger CI, etc.):
git commit --allow-empty -m "Trigger CI"
snippets.explanation: Amend is for the *latest* commit only, and only safe before push. After push you need a new commit (or a force-push, which has its own rules).

snippets.title: Branching
snippets.code:
git branch                   # list local branches (* marks current)
git branch -a                # include remote-tracking branches

git switch -c feature/auth   # create and check out a new branch off HEAD
git switch main              # switch back

git branch -d feature/auth   # delete a merged branch
git branch -D feature/auth   # force-delete an UNmerged branch (careful)
snippets.explanation: Modern Git: `switch` for moving between branches, `restore` for moving file content. `checkout` still works but does both jobs and is more confusing.

snippets.title: Configuring identity (one-time per machine)
snippets.code:
git config --global user.name  "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "you@example.com"

# Default the initial branch name to 'main':
git config --global init.defaultBranch main

# Show all config and where each value came from:
git config --list --show-origin
snippets.explanation: Identity is baked into every commit. Set it once at the global level; per-repo overrides via `git config user.email` (no `--global`).
